hook: Luca knocks on Agnes Ward's door, and the moment he says the words 'Cole House' she tries to close it in his face.
goal: Luca must convince Agnes Ward to tell him the truth about what her husband Edward did to the Cole family fifty years ago.

start_state.location: The doorstep of Agnes Ward's house, 11 Birch Street (loc_005)
items: handwritten note (Cole family left Harden July 1974; E. Ward)
knowledge: E. Ward was Edward Ward, who died two years ago
Agnes Ward is Edward's widow and still lives in Harden
The Cole family left Harden in July 1974 without warning
E. Ward was listed as a witness on the Cole House property sale
Ray Brennan stole the metal box from Luca's flat
Ray called Luca asking about 'value' on the same morning the box was found
suspicions: Ray and Edward Ward had a connection that links them both to the Cole family's departure
Agnes Ward may know what her late husband did
The box contains something that proves wrongdoing by Edward Ward or Ray Brennan

continuity_rules: Must open with Luca arriving at 11 Birch Street — not at the library or in transit
Must not repeat or re-dramatise the library discoveries from Episode 4
Must not contradict established facts: Ray has the box, Edward Ward died two years ago, the Cole family left in July 1974
Agnes Ward was named in Episode 4 — her appearance is not a surprise introduction
No new unresolved threads may be created — Episode 5 is past the thread-introduction cutoff
key_events: Luca arrives at 11 Birch Street and knocks; Agnes opens the door — her expression shows a flicker of recognition before Luca speaks (dramatic irony: Ray already came to warn her, but Luca does not know this); the moment Luca says he worked at the Cole House, Agnes grips the door and begins to close it.

Luca raises the video camera and says he found it inside the Cole House; Agnes goes still, stares at the camera, and after a long pause steps back and lets him in without a word — the camera means something to her.

Inside, Luca plays the tape on the small screen; Agnes watches in silence; then she says quietly, 'That is Edward. My husband.' Her hands tremble; she tells Luca what Edward confessed to her before he died two years ago: he borrowed a large sum of money from the Cole family, signed a document promising to repay it, but instead used his connections to force them out of Harden so the debt would never be collected; he hid the box — containing the signed document and part of the original cash — in the Cole House because he feared the family would one day return.

Luca asks about Ray Brennan; Agnes confirms that Ray was Edward's closest friend and knew everything; she then tells Luca that two days ago Ray came to her door and said someone had found the box — and in that moment Luca understands that Ray stole the box not for himself but to destroy the document inside before it could surface; Agnes gives Luca the address of Ray's office in town and says, very quietly, that she is tired of carrying the secret.
major_reveal: Edward Ward borrowed a large sum of money from the Cole family, signed a document as proof of the debt, then used his local connections to force the Cole family out of Harden rather than repay what he owed; the metal box holds that signed document and part of the original cash; Ray Brennan has known this all along and stole the box to destroy the evidence before it could be used.
tone_guardrails: Agnes's confession must feel sad and exhausted, not theatrical — she is a woman who has waited two years for this and is relieved as much as she is ashamed
The revelation is one connected truth — the debt, the document, and Ray's knowledge are parts of the same secret, not three separate shocks stacked together
Keep the scene physically anchored: the camera screen, the tape playing, Agnes's shaking hands, the quiet room
No new mystery is introduced — this is the climax; the episode closes questions, not opens them
forbidden_moves: Do not re-explain what was already found in Episodes 3 and 4 — Agnes may mention the Cole family or the property sale in passing, but must not retell what the reader already knows
Do not reveal where the Cole family's descendants are now — that discovery belongs to Episode 6
Do not bring Ray Brennan on stage — maximum two characters this episode: Luca and Agnes
Do not introduce any new unanswered question — the thread window is closed
ending_hook: Luca walks away from Agnes Ward's house knowing the full truth — but the signed document and the cash, the only physical proof, are locked inside the box, and Ray Brennan has the box at his office; Luca must reach Ray before Ray destroys the evidence.
